findings.title: Dynamic sampler flag transaction is untested
findings.impact: The new dynamic update path validates, commits, rolls back, and publishes request/access/diagnostic sampling rates as one transaction, but the diff only tests LogSampler::UpdateConfigFromFlags directly. A regression in UpdateFlagParameter, ValidateAndCommitSamplerFlags, rollback after a bad value, or explicit-state preservation would not be caught, so runtime config changes could silently leave clients/workers with the wrong sampling policy.
findings.fix: Add a flags-level test that calls Flags::UpdateFlagParameter with sampler entries, asserts the gflag values and LogSampler snapshot change together, and covers invalid batch rollback plus the request-only derivation/explicit override cases through this production path.

findings.title: EmbeddedConfig sample-rate setters have no regression coverage
findings.impact: RequestSampleRate, AccessSampleRate, and DiagnosticSampleRate add the public embedded-worker configuration surface, including range filtering before values enter extraArgs, but no changed test exercises these setters through EmbeddedConfig or ParseCommandLineFlags. A typo in the flag names, invalid-rate handling, or failure to propagate embedded config to the sampler would pass the current sampler-only tests.
findings.fix: Add an embedded-config/flag parsing test that builds an EmbeddedConfig with valid and invalid sample rates, parses it through the existing embedded flag path, and asserts the resulting FLAGS_* values and LogSampler effective ppm/derivation behavior.
